items.summary: Warsaw-based small modular reactor (SMR) developer SGE has unveiled plans to deploy 14 GE Vernova Hitachi Nuclear Energy (GVH) BWRX-300 units across three U.K. sites. items.title: Proposed rules on ALARA, reactor licensing revamp introduced by NRC
items.summary: A proposed rule from the Nuclear Regulatory Commission would update radiation protection regulations and remove “as low as reasonably achievable” (ALARA) language without changing specific radiation exposure limits that are based on the linear no-threshold (LNT) model of low-dose radiation health ef

items.title: British researchers test concrete method for Sr-90 treatment
items.summary: Researchers from the University of Manchester, the U.K. National Nuclear Laboratory, and Clemson University have studied using crushed concrete at legacy nuclear facilities as a long-term sink for strontium-90, a radioactive contaminant found at many such sites. items.title: Deployable Energy’s Unity Nuclear Reactor Achieves Criticality at INL, Third Under DOE Nuclear …
items.summary: Deployable Energy’s Unity demonstration reactor has achieved criticality at Idaho National Laboratory (INL), making it the third Department of Energy (DOE)–authorized advanced reactor to reach the milestone ahead of the July 4 deadline set under President Trump’s May 2025 nuclear executive order. items.title: Clinch River construction permit recommendation follows safety evaluation
items.summary: Staff at the Nuclear Regulatory Commission have recommended the agency issue a construction permit to the Tennessee Valley Authority for its plans to construct a GE Vernova Hitachi Nuclear Energy (GVH) BWRX-300 reactor at the Clinch River site in Tennessee, according to the safety evaluation report

items.title: EUROfusion steps up to lead Europe’s biggest science conversation
items.summary: From 1 July, EUROfusion will assume the presidency of EIROforum, a collective of Europe's largest scientific research organisations. Over the coming year, EUROfusion will chair the forum and help steer discussions on key priorities shaping the future of science in Europe - from the next Multiannua

items.title: ICYMI: Nuclear developments around the nation this month
items.summary: Updates from utility companies in Colorado and Arizona, nuclear legislation and discussions, and the potential Duane Arnold restart were among the news items in the month of June at the local and state levels.

Completing an international commitment between the two countries, the United Kingdom’s Nuclear Transport Solutions (NTS) delivered the third and final shipment of vitrified high-level radioactive waste to Germany, the U.K.

items.summary: Dena VolovarConditions for nuclear energy haven’t looked this favorable in a generation. Policy support is building, public support has risen, and demand for reliable power continues to outpace what other sources can provide. items.title: INL hosts talk of nuclear successes, next criticalities
items.summary: For just over a year, President Trump’s Executive Order 14301, “Reforming Nuclear Reactor Testing at the Department of Energy,” has loomed large because it pegged a stretch goal to a significant date: July 4, 2026. Will there be at least three participants in the Department of Energy’s Reactor Pilot

items.title: NRC to issue new Part 61 rules for low-level waste disposal
items.summary: Continuing its breakneck pace of introducing new rules and proposals, the Nuclear Regulatory Commission last week announced that it was proposing to amend its regulations under 10 CFR Part 61 governing the land disposal of low-level radioactive waste.The proposed rule, which introduces a new risk-in

items.title: DOE to Offer $17.5 Billion in Supply Chain Loans
items.summary: DOE to Offer $17.5 Billion in Supply Chain Loans Financing will help rebuild America’s nuclear supply chain and accelerate deployment of 10 new large-scale reactors. The U.S. Department of Energy’s (DOE) Office of Energy Dominance Financing (EDF) has issued a … Continue reading →

items.title: DOME: Legacy built, future ready
items.summary: In 2019, a familiar landmark at Idaho National Laboratory was scheduled for demolition. Though striking for both its physical presence and its significance to nuclear history, the containment dome that once housed Experimental Breeder Reactor-II sat unused—that is, until INL realized its potential a

items.title: Swedish state taking majority stake in Videberg Kraft
items.summary: The Swedish government has decided that the state will take a 60% stake in Videberg Kraft AB, which plans to build three Rolls-Royce small modular reactors on the Värö Peninsula near Ringhals.

items.title: First Lightbridge fuel samples removed from INL's Advanced Test Reactor
items.summary: Advanced nuclear fuel developer Lightbridge Corporation has announced that the first batch of its fuel material samples have been successfully removed from Idaho National Laboratory's Advanced Test Reactor after undergoing irradiation.

items.title: OSGE signs agreement with Włocławek for SMR plant
items.summary: The City of Włocławek and Orlen Synthos Green Energy have signed an agreement defining the principles of cooperation in connection with the construction of up to six BWRX-300 small modular reactors in the central Polish city.

items.title: UK fusion sector poised for industrial scale-up
items.summary: The UK fusion energy sector is entering a decisive transition from laboratory development to industrial-scale delivery, driven by rising private investment, sustained government backing, and an evolving regulatory framework designed to support deployment, speakers said at the NextGen Nuclear Summit,

items.title: US federal loan to jumpstart AP1000 reactor supply chain
items.summary: The US Department of Energy has conditionally committed to USD17.5 billion in loans to finance the purchase of long-lead items for up to 10 Westinghouse AP1000 reactors.

items.title: AtkinsRéalis kicks off US licensing for CANDU technology
items.summary: The sole licensee of the Canadian CANDU pressurised heavy water reactor technology has formally submitted a Notice of Intent to the US Nuclear Regulatory Commission to begin licensing it in the USA.

items.title: Holtec and EDF submit UK SMR project proposal
items.summary: US-based Holtec International and France-based EDF have submitted a joint proposal for up to four SMR-300 small modular reactors at the Cottam power plant site in Nottinghamshire in the UK.

items.title: Walmart signs nuclear power purchase agreement with Constellation
items.summary: Retail giant Walmart has signed a long-term power purchase agreement for emissions-free electricity from Constellation's Dresden nuclear power plant in Illinois in the USA.

items.title: Russia planning 'high-capacity' nuclear fuel reprocessing plant
items.summary: Investment feasibility and site selection studies are taking place, with a decision due by the end of 2026, for a reprocessing plant for used nuclear fuel with an initial capacity of 400 tonnes per year.

items.title: Canada aims to grow nuclear and uranium capacity, at home and abroad
items.summary: The first-ever national nuclear strategy released by the Canadian government outlines a vision of new nuclear builds, as well as expanded uranium production, and a strengthened supply chain, as the nation works towards its ambition to become an "energy superpower".

items.title: Major component milestone for Indian reactor
items.summary: Nuclear Power Corporation of India Ltd says the successful unloading of the first End Shield is a major milestone for the Kaiga 5 and 6 construction project.

items.title: Fusion supply chain spend up, but challenges remain
items.summary: The Fusion Industry Association has reported that supply chain spending by the fusion industry rose 25% to USD538 million in 2025, but ensuring access to specialist materials and fuel systems remains a continuing concern.

items.title: IAEA produces global mapping tool of used nuclear fuel
items.summary: The global total of used nuclear fuel produced by nuclear power plants is about 448,000 tonnes of heavy metal, with three quarters in storage and one quarter reprocessed, according to the International Atomic Energy Agency's first interactive tool showing how much used nuclear fuel the world has - a

items.title: Valar Atomics achieves criticality in DOE Reactor Pilot Program
items.summary: Valar Atomics' Ward 250 advanced reactor has completed a zero-power fuelled criticality demonstration, becoming the second to meet the 4 July deadline for the US Department of Energy’s Reactor Pilot Program.
